blocks.content: Lyria pulled out a totem and held it out.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: While the mental entity remains, we'll isolate this area with the law. We need to set up a golden formation, so help me out. You know how to calculate a three-dimensional magic square, right?
Dante shrugged nonchalantly.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Well... it's kind of my specialty.
As someone who specialized in magic circles, he was more than qualified to assist Lyria with her work.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: However, Dante still had doubts.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Would this really be enough? Even if they isolated the mental entity in the old ruins, trapping it in a sealing formation afterward was a completely different matter.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Can we finish the golden formation by tomorrow morning?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: It depends on our skill, but if we split the work, it should be possible. Why?
Dante glanced back at the still-lit shopping district.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: There were probably countless couples wandering the streets, drawn to the lights like moths.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I think we might need some reinforcements.
After parting ways with Dante, Shirone and Amy spoke much less than before.
But the atmosphere wasn't tense.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy was preoccupied with Dante's advice, pondering how to find her own style.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, too, was deep in thought, struggling to fully control the wrathful spirit within him.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That was really dangerous earlier.
Armand had broken free of his control and targeted someone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It wasn't that Shirone lacked mental discipline. It was more that his anger had grown stronger than before.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, it's natural for one's personality to change over time. But this wasn't the direction he wanted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I need more training. I'll visit Teacher Etella during the break and ask for advice.
Having reached that conclusion, Shirone found the Snow Crystal Inn that Shakora had recommended.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is the place I mentioned.
Amy didn't say anything, just observing the atmosphere from outside.
Shirone examined the exterior of the building.
As Shakora had said, the ambiance was nice. The decorations were elegant, and the view of the endless snowy landscape was pleasing.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why insist on this place?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well, I heard the atmosphere is really nice. We can relax here.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Alright...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Though practical by nature, this trip was also meant to console her, so she appreciated the effort he had put in.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Still, she felt a bit guilty.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: On a day like this, it would have been nice to spend the night together, but her mind was too conflicted.
As they entered, Shirone was surprised by the bright lights.
Numerous lamps lit up the hall as if it were daytime.
Past a right-angled corridor was a spacious terrace where tourists gathered around a hearth, drinking tea.
A minstrel's music filled the air.

blocks.speaker: Receptionist
blocks.content: Are you two together?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, two of us.
While Shirone arranged the rooms, Amy stood a bit apart, looking elsewhere.
It felt strange for a man and woman to enter such a place together, unlike traveling with friends in a group.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Two rooms, please.
The receptionist glanced between Shirone and Amy, then nodded and placed the keys on the table.

blocks.speaker: Receptionist
blocks.content: Alright, rooms 311 and 312. Take the stairs to the third floor and turn right in the corridor. Payment is in advance.
After paying, Shirone looked at Amy, who slowly walked towards the stairs.
As they walked down the third-floor corridor, Shirone spoke.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's unpack first. If you're hungry, we can go out to eat.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No, I just want to rest today. Walking around in the cold has made me feel heavy. Is that okay?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If she was truly tired, resting was fine, but Shirone felt she might be lying.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe she was angrier than he thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy, about earlier...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't misunderstand. It's not that. You know I don't hold grudges.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt even more uneasy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The fact that she mentioned grudges suggested she did hold one.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Alright, see you tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah, you rest too.
Amy unlocked her door and went inside.
Shirone watched her go, then inserted his key into the lock.
Entering his room, the first thing he noticed was the endless snowy landscape through the large window.
In the distance, tiny torches flickered, and people were ice fishing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How nice it would have been to see this view with Amy.
The thought made him sigh.
Sigh.
Sigh.
Shirone and Amy turned to look at each other simultaneously.
Then, as if on cue, they turned towards the door.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is this?
There were two doors but only one room.
And only one bed.
They stared at each other in confusion.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Most importantly, he had insisted on this inn, so he needed to come up with an excuse.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What should I do? Should I ask to change rooms? But all the rooms here might be like this. Should we just get a refund and leave? Would Amy be upset? Or would she prefer that?
